name: Ticket Code, QR, and Delivery Token Foundation
primary_area: Tickets / Security / Token Foundation / QR Payloads
implementation_code_allowed: yes; ticket code, QR payload, delivery token, token hash primitives, tests, and missing TicketIssue token indexes only; no issuing, Attendee mutation, scanner/mobile sync, payment/order/inventory/delivery/WhatsApp behavior
existing_systems_forbidden_from_mutation: Scanner routes and scanner hot path

ticket_code.purpose: human/support reference and durable unique ticket identifier

qr_token.purpose: scanner QR payload secret where scanner model supports opaque token

required_indexes: unique(qr_token_hash) where qr_token_hash is not null
unique(delivery_token_hash) where delivery_token_hash is not null
index(delivery_token_expires_at) where delivery_token_hash is not null
forbidden_changes: scanner route or scanner hot-path change
using delivery_token as QR scanner token
using ticket_code alone as secure ticket-page bearer token

ticket_code: RED ticket code is sequential or derived from DB/order/customer/provider data
GREEN ticket code is random, non-sequential, URL/QR safe, and >=128 bits entropy
RED ticket_code unique DB constraint is missing
qr_payload: RED QR payload includes PII, order id, payment reference, or delivery URL
GREEN QR payload includes only approved version prefix and opaque code/token
RED parser accepts malformed payload silently
GREEN parser returns explicit ok/error tuples
RED VS-08 modifies scanner or Android scanner behavior
GREEN no scanner/mobile code paths are modified
delivery_token: RED plaintext delivery_token is stored or logged
GREEN only delivery_token_hash is persisted/logged in redacted form
RED token hash uses plain SHA256 without a secret
GREEN hash uses secret-backed HMAC or approved helper
RED delivery token has too little entropy
GREEN delivery token uses >=256 bits randomness
RED missing token secret uses insecure default
GREEN missing token secret fails closed in production
indexes_and_lookup: RED delivery_token_hash lookup would scan table or compare plaintext
GREEN hash-first indexed lookup contract is present
RED qr_token_hash partial unique index is absent
GREEN qr_token_hash partial unique index exists where field exists
RED delivery_token_hash partial unique index is absent
GREEN delivery_token_hash partial unique index exists where field exists
boundary_security: RED VS-08 creates TicketIssue/Attendee/DeliveryAttempt records or enqueues issuance/delivery workers
GREEN VS-08 only provides generator/hash/payload/index foundations
RED logs or telemetry include plaintext token, raw bearer QR payload, PII, provider payload, or secret
GREEN logs and telemetry contain only sanitized metadata

hot_data: plaintext tokens in memory only during generation/render/send
cold_data: TicketIssue token hashes and expiry metadata in Postgres
redis_structures: none required in VS-08; future secure ticket rate limiting may use sorted sets

ttl_strategy.delivery_token: expires via delivery_token_expires_at; exact duration must follow VS-00B security policy
ttl_strategy.qr_token: no Redis TTL; revoked via TicketIssue state/hash semantics

invalidation_triggers: TicketIssue token rotation invalidates secure ticket page cache in future VS-11
TicketIssue revocation/refund/cancellation invalidates ticket page and scanner-facing caches in future VS-15A
Delivery token expiry must prevent future cached access
pubsub_rules: none required in VS-08; future broadcasts must never include plaintext tokens
indexes_required: unique(qr_token_hash) where qr_token_hash is not null
unique(delivery_token_hash) where delivery_token_hash is not null
index(delivery_token_expires_at) where delivery_token_hash is not null

next_slice: VS-09A — Ticket Issuance Contract and Idempotency Model
